ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Indomie-style instant noodles with egg, hotdog, carrot and corn specifically. FOODON_00002473 (instant noodle food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (Noodles, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Indomie-style instant noodles with mixed toppings.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific instant noodle dish with mixed toppings.

allergens: may contain traces of shellfish and milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Super Saver Indomie is approximately 380 g, comprising one packet of Indomie instant noodles (85 g dry weight, approximately 170 g cooked), one fried egg (~50 g), two hotdog slices (~60 g), a portion of sliced carrots (~50 g cooked), and sweet corn kernels (~50 g cooked), as typically served at The Noodle Place, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Indomie Mi Goreng Instant Noodles (cooked, with seasoning) and component ingredients estimated from USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact restaurant dish. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted-average composition of cooked Indomie mi goreng noodles (USDA FDC #2103688 and analogous HPB entries for instant noodles), fried egg (USDA FDC #748967), pork/chicken hotdog sausage (USDA FDC #174608), cooked carrot (USDA FDC #170393), and sweet corn kernels (USDA FDC #169998), combined in proportions consistent with the described 380 g serving. Values represent the blended per-100 g profile of the assembled dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of instant noodles and mixed noodle dishes — analogous estimate based on Indomie-style instant noodles (GI ~52) from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 52 for cooked instant noodles (wheat-based, boiled). This value is used as the analogous estimate for the noodle base of this dish. The addition of egg, hotdog, carrot and corn does not substantially alter the GI of the noodle component; the overall dish GI is estimated at 52 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 66.5) / 100 ≈ 34.6, where carbohydrate per 380 g serving ≈ 66.5 g (17.5 g per 100 g × 3.8).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from instant noodle seasoning sachet
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content (approximately 420 mg per 100 g, equating to ~1,596 mg per serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; requesting reduced seasoning or using only half the sachet can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined wheat-based instant noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that this dish carries a moderate-to-high glycemic load per serving (~34.6) driven by the refined wheat instant noodle base (GI ~52); portion control is recommended, and the egg, carrot and corn components provide some protein and fibre that may attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and saturated fat from seasoning sachet and processed hotdog
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish with caution due to its elevated sodium content and the presence of saturated fat (~1.9 g per 100 g) and trans fat (~0.1 g per 100 g) contributed by the instant noodle seasoning oil and processed hotdog sausage, which are risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Super Saver Indomie is a popular value-priced Indonesian instant noodle dish served at The Noodle Place on Circular Road, Singapore. The dish is built on a base of Indomie brand instant noodles — either the original mi goreng (stir-fried) or the special flavour variant — cooked and tossed with the included seasoning sachet containing palm oil, soy sauce, chilli, and dried shallots. The noodles are topped with a fried egg, sliced hotdog sausage, julienned or sliced carrots, and sweet corn kernels, making it a filling and affordable meal. The texture is soft and slightly chewy from the noodles, with a savoury, mildly spicy flavour profile from the seasoning. The egg adds richness, the hotdog contributes a smoky-salty note, and the carrot and corn provide mild sweetness and colour. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate source of protein (~6.2 g per 100 g) and carbohydrate (~17.5 g per 100 g), with a notable sodium load (~420 mg per 100 g) from the seasoning sachet. The glycemic index of the noodle base is estimated at 52 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full serving is substantial at approximately 34.6. Clinically, the dish is of concern for individuals managing hypertension due to its high sodium content, and requires portion awareness for those with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the egg and hotdog components. The dish is widely recognised in Singapore as a comfort food and budget meal option, reflecting the cultural integration of Indonesian Indomie noodles into the Singaporean hawker and casual dining landscape.

Indomie goreng with egg nutrition facts

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this exact restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of component ingredients using USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) and HPB Singapore Foodo data for analogous items. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-based instant noodles. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and based on approved sources, but the exact dish formulation and seasoning quantities at The Noodle Place are not independently verified.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component ingredients (cooked Indomie mi goreng noodles, fried egg, hotdog sausage, cooked carrot, sweet corn) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and HPB Foodo data, combined in proportions consistent with the described 380 g serving. No single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish. 2. Sodium content is high primarily due to the Indomie seasoning sachet; actual sodium may vary if the restaurant uses partial seasoning. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component ingredient sources used for this estimation. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not consistently reported in the component ingredient sources used. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used for this estimation. 6. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-based instant noodles (boiled/cooked); the dish GI may be slightly lower due to the protein and fibre contributions of egg, carrot and corn. 7. Halal status is based on Indomie brand products being certified halal by MUI (Indonesia) and widely sold as halal in Singapore; hotdog sausage halal status depends on the specific product used by the restaurant and should be verified. 8. The food_name has been expanded from the menu label 'Super Saver Indomie' to 'Super Saver Indomie with Egg, Hotdog, Carrot and Corn' to reflect the described composition and improve canonical identity. 9. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g per 100 g is a low-level estimate from the palm oil fraction in the seasoning sachet; this value is plausible but not directly confirmed from a single source entry.
